title: Strength B
subtitle: Upper body + core
coaching_note: Monitor elbow throughout — the rehab exercises at the end are the fix, not just caution. Face pulls and band work protect the shoulder long-term.

exercises.name: Band Pull-Apart
exercises.form_cue: Arms straight. Pull band to chest, squeeze shoulder blades together at end. Control the return.
exercises.why: Activates rear delts and rotator cuff. Counteracts the shoulder internal rotation pattern from desk work and badminton.

exercises.name: Shoulder Circles
exercises.form_cue: Big slow circles forward and back. Let the shoulder blade move.
exercises.why: Shoulder and thoracic mobility before pressing.

exercises.name: Cat-Cow
exercises.form_cue: On all fours. Breathe in on cow (belly drops), breathe out on cat (spine rounds). Slow.
exercises.why: Thoracic mobility. Restricted thoracic spine forces the lumbar to compensate — this helps.

phases.name: Upper Body
phases.coaching_note: Monitor elbow throughout. Any sharp pain on these movements — stop, drop weight, reassess.

exercises.name: DB Floor Press
exercises.form_cue: Lying on floor, arms 45 degrees from body. Press up, don't let elbows flare wide. The floor limits depth — protects the shoulder at the bottom.
exercises.why: Chest and tricep strength without the shoulder strain of a bench. Floor limits depth, making it elbow-friendlier.

exercises.name: DB Bent-Over Row
exercises.form_cue: Hinge at hips, back flat. Pull DB to hip — not to chest. Elbow stays close to body. Squeeze the lat at the top.
exercises.why: Back and lat strength. Pulling strength balances all the pushing. Critical for posture.

exercises.name: Bent-Over Reverse Fly (light DB)
exercises.form_cue: Hinge forward at hips, back flat, arms hanging. Raise both arms out to the sides to shoulder height, slight bend in elbow. Squeeze shoulder blades together at the top. Use 2-3kg max.
exercises.why: Rear delt and rotator cuff strength. Same target as face pulls, no anchor needed. Counters badminton's internal rotation load.

exercises.name: Lateral Raise (pilates band)
exercises.form_cue: Stand on band. Arms slightly in front of body, slight bend in elbow. Raise to shoulder height only. Don't shrug.
exercises.why: Side delt strength for shoulder stability. Using the light pilates band keeps load off the elbow.

exercises.name: Push-Up
exercises.form_cue: Hands just outside shoulders. Body straight line. Lower chest to floor, press all the way up. Stop 2 reps before failure — don't grind.
exercises.why: Bodyweight pressing strength. AMRAP means go until form breaks, not until you collapse.

exercises.name: Y Raise (light DB)
exercises.form_cue: Slight forward lean. Arms form a Y at 45 degrees, thumbs up. Raise to shoulder height only. Use 2-3kg max.
exercises.why: Targets lower trapezius and posterior deltoid — prevents shoulder impingement on overhead movements.

exercises.name: Side-Lying External Rotation (ankle weight)
exercises.form_cue: Lie on your side. Elbow bent 90 degrees, upper arm pinned to side. Hold ankle weight in top hand. Rotate forearm up toward ceiling, lower slowly. Keep the elbow glued.
exercises.why: Rotator cuff strengthening — builds the stabilizers that protect the shoulder during badminton overheads. Uses ankle weight (1kg) instead of band.

exercises.name: Hollow Hold
exercises.form_cue: On back. Arms overhead, legs raised. Lower back pressed to floor. If lower back arches, tuck knees in.
exercises.why: Full anterior chain tension. Carries over to running posture.

exercises.name: Side Plank
exercises.form_cue: Hip stacked. Don't let it sag. Breathe normally.
exercises.why: Lateral core — protecting that left iliac crest.

exercises.name: Dead Bug
exercises.form_cue: Lower back stays flat. Extend opposite arm and leg slowly. Return slowly.
exercises.why: Deep core stability to end the session.

phases.name: Elbow Rehab
phases.coaching_note: Even when tired, do the eccentric curls. That slow lowering is what heals the tendon.

exercises.name: Wrist Flexor Stretch
exercises.form_cue: Arm straight, palm up. Gently pull fingers back with other hand. Feel the stretch on the inside of the forearm. Don't force it.
exercises.why: Lengthens the wrist flexors and medial forearm tendons — addresses the golfer's elbow tension site.

exercises.name: Eccentric Wrist Curl
exercises.form_cue: Forearm on thigh, palm up, 2-3kg DB. Use other hand to lift the weight up. Lower slowly with working arm only — 3 counts down. That slow lowering is the whole point.
exercises.why: Eccentric loading is the gold-standard treatment for golfer's elbow. The slow lowering phase remodels the tendon.

exercises.name: Reverse Wrist Curl
exercises.form_cue: Forearm on thigh, palm down, 2-3kg DB. Curl knuckles up toward you, lower slowly. Don't let the forearm lift off the thigh.
exercises.why: Strengthens the wrist extensors — the antagonist to the injured flexor group. Balanced forearm strength reduces tendon stress.

progression_notes: Push-ups: when you can consistently hit 15+ reps per set, add a resistance band across your back for extra load. DB exercises: add 1kg per side every 1-2 weeks when all reps are clean.
